steps.title: Identify the per-acre estimate
steps.content:
The arborist estimates there are at least 50 and no more than 60 hickory trees per acre. This translates to an inequality:
$$50 \leq \text{trees per acre} \leq 60$$

steps.title: Scale the inequality for 3 acres
steps.content:
To find the total number of trees in a 3-acre section, multiply all parts of the inequality by 3:
$$50 \times 3 \leq x \leq 60 \times 3$$
$$150 \leq x \leq 180$$
This matches choice B — B is correct.
